name: United States v. Denedo
question: Does the Navy-Marine Corps Court of Criminal Appeals retain jurisdiction under 28 U.S.C. Section 1651, the All Writs Act, to consider a writ of error coram nobis sought by a former service member?
description: A case in which the Court held that the Navy-Marine Corps Court of Criminal Appeals (NMCCA) retained jurisdiction under the All Writs Act to consider coram nobis petitions that alleged a prior judgment of conviction was fundamentally flawed.
